text: The title compound, (NBu4)2Fe(III)2(dhbq)3 (dhbq(2-/3-) = 2,5-dioxidobenzoquinone/1,2-dioxido-4,5-semiquinone), is a three-dimensional network solid composed of paramagnetic iron(III) sites and electron-delocalized semiquinoid ligands. Electrical conductivity at ambient temperature is 0.16 ± 0.01 S/cm, one of the highest reported to date for a metal-organic framework. Analysis of magnetic and conductivity behavior in conjunction with detailed spectra determined from X-Band EPR (0.9 K to 300 K), UV-vis-NIR DF (25 K to 325 K), and slow-scan cyclic voltammetry reveal the networks to be electronically conductive based solely upon ligand mixed-valency. This is demonstrated to be a novel observation by the UV-vis-NIR DF measurements of the title compound, which become the first-in-class to display Robin-Day Class II/III mixed valency in a MOF. In an investigation to understand the effects of modifying ligand redox properties on MOF properties, Na0.9(NBu4)1.8Fe(III)2(dhbq)3 is synthesized in lesser solvent and shows significant different magnitudes of conductivity and distinct values of the magnetic ordering temperatures for both species.
